behavior_summary: This script manages the lifecycle of visual/behavioral sub-processes (body-part processes) for the left-arm object.
U8P_ARMLEFT::use (Event 1) marks the object with a process-info/process-exclude state and spawns a BODYPART process (class 0x0270) associated with the object, returning the spawned handle.

U8P_ARMLEFT::hit (Event 5) marks the object with a different process-info/process-exclude state, calls FREE::2A56 to remove a related resource/process (ProcessType 0x0202) for the object, then queries Kernel::getNumProcesses for ProcessType 0x020A. If no processes of that type remain, it spawns a fallback/supplemental BODYPART process (class 0x0431).
notable_control_flow: U8P_ARMLEFT::use always sets process-info/process-exclude and then spawns BODYPART::0270; the spawn retval is captured into a local temp and returned (direct create-and-return flow).
U8P_ARMLEFT::hit performs cleanup (FREE::2A56) then conditionally spawns BODYPART::0431 only when Kernel::getNumProcesses returns zero for the checked ProcessType (guarded fallback spawn).
open_questions: Exact semantic meaning of numeric process-info identifiers and process-type codes (e.g., 0x0207, 0x020B, 0x0202, 0x020A) — they are inferred from call patterns but not documented here.
Precise effect of the FREE::2A56 call: appears to remove/unregister a related process/resource for the entity but internal semantics (destroy, detach, decrement reference) are not certain.
Whether the indirection '2-byte deref from [BP+06h]' represents an item PID, entity handle, or a nested object pointer in all use-cases — inference is strong but not absolutely proven.
What in-game visuals/animations the spawned classes 0x0270 and 0x0431 correspond to (assumed body-part visual/effect but unknown).

main_function.name: U8P_ARMLEFT::use (Event 1)
main_function.purpose: Handle the player/actor 'use' (activate) event on the left-arm object by marking the object with process-info/process-exclude and spawning a BODYPART process (class 0x0270) tied to that object; capture and return the spawned process handle.
entry_conditions: Event 1: Use/activate event invoked when the actor/avatar uses or activates this object.

function_map.name: U8P_ARMLEFT::use (Event 1)
function_map.role: On-use handler: set process info/exclude and spawn primary BODYPART process (class 0x0270); returns spawned handle.

function_map.name: U8P_ARMLEFT::hit (Event 5)
function_map.role: On-hit handler: set process info/exclude, free a related resource/process (FREE::2A56), check remaining processes of a type, and conditionally spawn fallback BODYPART (class 0x0431) if none remain.

function_map.name: spawn BODYPART::0270
function_map.role: Spawn/instantiate a body-part process class (used by use handler).

function_map.name: FREE::2A56
function_map.role: Free/unregister a resource/process associated with an entity and ProcessType (used by hit handler).

function_map.name: Kernel::getNumProcesses
function_map.role: Query the kernel for the number of running processes of a given ProcessType for the target entity (used as a guard in hit handler).

function_map.name: spawn BODYPART::0431
function_map.role: Spawn fallback/supplemental body-part process when no processes of the checked type remain (used by hit handler).

external_calls.purpose: Create/attach a body-part process instance associated with the object after setting process-info/exclude; retval is captured and returned.
Remove or unregister a related process/resource for the entity using ProcessType 0x0202 — cleanup step before evaluating whether to spawn fallback.
Obtain the count of processes of ProcessType 0x020A for the entity; used to decide whether to spawn fallback BODYPART::0431 when count is zero.
Spawn a fallback/supplemental body-part process only if Kernel::getNumProcesses reports zero remaining processes of the checked type.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script manages the lifecycle of visual/behavioral sub-processes (body-part processes) for the left-arm object.
